blocks.task: Implement FlashAttention class with tiled computation and online softmax, add comprehensive tests
blocks.summary: Flash Attention implemented. FlashAttention class + standalone flashAttention() function. Tiled Q×K computation with online softmax trick — O(N·d) memory vs O(N²). Causal mask support. Backward pass with recomputation. 15 tests passing, exact numerical match against standard attention. Gradient check passes.
blocks.details: Flash Attention implemented. FlashAttention class + standalone flashAttention() function. Tiled Q×K computation with online softmax trick — O(N·d) memory vs O(N²). Causal mask support. Backward pass with recomputation. 15 tests passing, exact numerical match against standard attention. Gradient check passes.

blocks.task: Stress-test HenryDB Volcano integration: adversarial queries, edge cases, performance
blocks.summary: Stress-tested HenryDB Volcano integration with 15 adversarial queries: NULL join keys, empty tables, 1000×500 large joins (8ms), self-join, multi-join, cross join, window+join, CTE+join, FULL JOIN+COALESCE. All work correctly. Found one minor bug: ORDER BY prefers original column over SELECT alias when both have same name (COALESCE alias vs raw column). Not critical, noted for future fix.
blocks.details: Stress-tested HenryDB Volcano integration with 15 adversarial queries: NULL join keys, empty tables, 1000×500 large joins (8ms), self-join, multi-join, cross join, window+join, CTE+join, FULL JOIN+COALESCE. blocks.task: HenryDB: Fix ORDER BY alias resolution — prefer SELECT aliases over table columns
blocks.summary: Root cause: ORDER BY sorts BEFORE SELECT projection. So ORDER BY product resolves to raw s.product (from join) not the COALESCE alias. Fix: resolve ORDER BY columns against SELECT aliases when the column name matches an alias. Adjust _orderByValue to accept selectCols and check aliases first.
blocks.details: Root cause: ORDER BY sorts BEFORE SELECT projection. So ORDER BY product resolves to raw s.product (from join) not the COALESCE alias. Fix: resolve ORDER BY columns against SELECT aliases when the column name matches an alias. Adjust _orderByValue to accept selectCols and check aliases first.

blocks.task: Fix ORDER BY to resolve SELECT aliases before raw columns, add test for COALESCE alias ordering
blocks.summary: Fixed ORDER BY alias resolution. Root cause: _selectInner checked raw column in row before alias expression map. Swapped priority so COALESCE/function aliases are preferred. Added _preComputeOrderByAliases for second exec path. 7 new tests all pass.
blocks.details: Fixed ORDER BY alias resolution. Root cause: _selectInner checked raw column in row before alias expression map. Swapped priority so COALESCE/function aliases are preferred. Added _preComputeOrderByAliases for second exec path. 7 new tests all pass.

blocks.task: Performance comparison: FlashAttention vs SelfAttention at various sequence lengths
blocks.summary: Performance comparison: Flash attention is 1-3x faster for small seqLens (8-64) due to cache effects, roughly equivalent for larger (128-256). Memory is the real win: 5.3x reduction at seqLen=128, 124x at seqLen=2048 (32MB→264KB). In JS without GPU SRAM, the compute is the same O(N²d) but memory savings prevent OOM for long sequences.
blocks.details: Performance comparison: Flash attention is 1-3x faster for small seqLens (8-64) due to cache effects, roughly equivalent for larger (128-256). Memory is the real win: 5.3x reduction at seqLen=128, 124x at seqLen=2048 (32MB→264KB). In JS without GPU SRAM, the compute is the same O(N²d) but memory savings prevent OOM for long sequences.

blocks.summary: Plan: In _selectInner SELECT * path, detect when multiple qualified keys resolve to same unqualified name. For collisions, use qualified names (a.id, b.id). For unique names, use unqualified.
blocks.details: Plan: In _selectInner SELECT * path, detect when multiple qualified keys resolve to same unqualified name. For collisions, use qualified names (a.id, b.id). For unique names, use unqualified.

blocks.task: HenryDB: Find the worst-performing TPC-H query and optimize it via Volcano
blocks.summary: TPC-H benchmark: Q1-Q10 all pass, 5-25ms range. Q3 (25ms) is slowest: Limit→Sort→Project→HashAggregate→Filter→HashJoin×2→SeqScan×3. Filter not pushed to scans (optimization opportunity). EXPLAIN FORMAT VOLCANO provides clear visibility into execution plans.
blocks.details: TPC-H benchmark: Q1-Q10 all pass, 5-25ms range. Q3 (25ms) is slowest: Limit→Sort→Project→HashAggregate→Filter→HashJoin×2→SeqScan×3. Filter not pushed to scans (optimization opportunity). EXPLAIN FORMAT VOLCANO provides clear visibility into execution plans.

blocks.task: HenryDB: Predicate pushdown into Volcano SeqScan — push WHERE conditions into scan filter
blocks.summary: Plan: split WHERE conjuncts by table reference. Conjuncts referencing only one table get pushed into a Filter wrapping that SeqScan. Cross-table conjuncts stay in a Filter above the join. Use AST walking to determine which tables each conjunct references.
blocks.details: Plan: split WHERE conjuncts by table reference. Conjuncts referencing only one table get pushed into a Filter wrapping that SeqScan. Cross-table conjuncts stay in a Filter above the join. Use AST walking to determine which tables each conjunct references.

blocks.task: Implement predicate pushdown in volcano-planner: split WHERE into per-table conditions and push to SeqScans, verify with EXPLAIN FORMAT VOLCANO
blocks.summary: Predicate pushdown in Volcano planner. Splits WHERE conjuncts by table reference, pushes single-table conditions into Filter wrapping SeqScan. Cross-table conditions stay as residual. Verified with EXPLAIN FORMAT VOLCANO showing correct plan changes. 238 tests pass.
blocks.details: Predicate pushdown in Volcano planner. Splits WHERE conjuncts by table reference, pushes single-table conditions into Filter wrapping SeqScan. Cross-table conditions stay as residual. Verified with EXPLAIN FORMAT VOLCANO showing correct plan changes. 238 tests pass.

blocks.task: Neural-net: Grouped Query Attention (GQA) — fewer K/V heads than Q heads, used in Llama 2/3
blocks.summary: Grouped Query Attention (GQA) implemented. Fewer KV heads than Q heads — used in Llama 2/3, Mistral. numKVHeads=numHeads → MHA, numKVHeads=1 → MQA. 4x KV cache reduction at 8Q/2KV heads. Uses flash attention per head. 8 tests passing, MHA equivalence verified.
blocks.details: Grouped Query Attention (GQA) implemented. Fewer KV heads than Q heads — used in Llama 2/3, Mistral. numKVHeads=numHeads → MHA, numKVHeads=1 → MQA. 4x KV cache reduction at 8Q/2KV heads. Uses flash attention per head. 8 tests passing, MHA equivalence verified.

queue.task: Session B kickoff: Review Session A's 29 bugs + 97% pass rate. Assess what's most impactful for depth work: CTEs through Volcano, correlated subqueries, window functions, or extraction.
queue.summary: Reviewed Session A: 29 bugs, 97%+ pass rate. Priority: CTEs through Volcano (14x speedup, high ROI), then correlated subqueries, then window functions. Queue aligned.

queue.goal: HenryDB: Route CTEs through Volcano for 14x speedup (5ms vs 70ms)
queue.summary: CTEs already route through Volcano planner (lines 28-56). Found real bug: CTE alias qualification broken (rs.region→undefined). Plan: fix alias bug + add tests.

queue.task: Fix CTE alias qualification bug: rs.region returns undefined when CTE aliased as rs
queue.summary: Fixed CTE alias qualification bug. CTE inner JOINs produced qualified names (c.region) that became double-qualified (rs.c.region) in outer query. Strip qualifiers during CTE materialization. 66 CTE tests + 85 SQL tests pass.

queue.task: Add comprehensive CTE-Volcano correctness tests (CTE aliasing, multi-CTE, nested CTE)
queue.summary: Added 11 CTE-Volcano integration tests: alias qualification, multi-CTE, JOINs inside CTEs, 3-way JOINs, parity checks. All pass.

queue.task: Git push, dashboard update, knowledge capture
queue.summary: Git pushed workspace+henrydb. Dashboard online and regenerated. CTE fix knowledge captured.

queue.task: Reflect on CTE work. Check remaining Volcano gaps. Decide next: window functions or correlated subqueries?
queue.summary: Correlated EXISTS/IN already work in Volcano. Real gap: scalar correlated subqueries in SELECT return null (Volcano), work in legacy. Also: func-wrapped aggregates (COALESCE(SUM(x),0)) fall back to legacy. Priority: (1) func-wrapped aggregates (quick win), (2) scalar correlated subqueries in SELECT.

queue.goal: HenryDB: Add correlated subquery support to Volcano planner
queue.summary: Two features: (1) func-wrapped aggregates: detect nested function(aggregate) in planner, extract inner agg, apply outer func in Project. (2) scalar correlated subqueries: buildValueGetter detect subquery type, execute sub-plan per outer row. 2 BUILD tasks.

queue.task: Support function-wrapped aggregates in Volcano (COALESCE(SUM(x),0), NULLIF(AVG(x),0), etc.)
queue.summary: Function-wrapped aggregates now supported in Volcano. COALESCE(SUM), NULLIF(COUNT), ROUND(AVG), ABS(SUM) all work. Guard removed. 115/115 tests pass.

queue.task: Support scalar correlated subqueries in Volcano SELECT columns
queue.summary: Scalar correlated subqueries now work in Volcano SELECT columns. Uses buildCorrelatedSubqueryPlan per outer row. Results match legacy exactly. 115/115 tests pass.
Git pushed henrydb + workspace. Dashboard synced.

queue.task: Mid-session quality check. Depth check: am I learning? Assess remaining time and priorities.
queue.summary: Depth check passed: CTE alias bug was surprising, func-wrapped agg fallback was educational. 4 BUILDs so far, good learning rate. Revised plan: drop window functions (too complex), add derived tables in FROM (common, achievable). 5.75h remaining.
